title: E-TTS: A New Embodied Test-Time Scaling Framework for Robotic Manipulation
abstract: arXiv:2606.27268v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Recently, a few works have made early attempts to study test-time scaling for embodied tasks. However, two major challenges remain unsolved: (1) reasoning can effectively improve the performance of the policy, but its scaling mechanism has seldom been studied; (2) historical information is essential, as embodied tasks are inherently long-horizon and sequential, making sole reliance on current observations for action scaling inadequate due to the lack of historical context utilization. To address these challenges, we introduce E-TTS, a modular and plug-and-play Embodied Test-Time Scaling framework that unifies reasoning and action scaling for robotic manipulation via history-aware iterative refinement with vision-language verifiers. To support joint reasoning-action scaling, E-TTS performs reasoning-action joint sampling and scoring in a pairwise manner. To better utilize historical information, E-TTS uses a history buffer to store hist

Title: E-TTS: A New Embodied Test-Time Scaling Framework for Robotic Manipulation
Abstract: arXiv:2606.27268v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Recently, a few works have made early attempts to study test-time scaling for embodied tasks. However, two major challenges remain unsolved: (1) reasoning can effectively improve the performance of the policy, but its scaling mechanism has seldom been studied; (2) historical information is essential, as embodied tasks are inherently long-horizon and sequential, making sole reliance on current observations for action scaling inadequate due to the lack of historical context utilization. To address these challenges, we introduce E-TTS, a modular and plug-and-play Embodied Test-Time Scaling framework that unifies reasoning and action scaling for robotic manipulation via history-aware iterative refinement with vision-language verifiers. To support joint reasoning-action scaling, E-TTS performs reasoning-action joint sampling and scoring in a pairwise manner. To better utilize historical information, E-TTS uses a history buffer to store hist
translated_title: E-TTS：一种用于机器人操作的新兴具身测试时扩展框架
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.27268v1 公告类型：交叉 摘要：近期，少数研究初步探索了具身任务中的测试时扩展。然而，仍有两个主要挑战未解决：（1）推理能有效提升策略性能，但其扩展机制鲜有研究；（2）历史信息至关重要，因为具身任务本质上是长期且连续的，仅依赖当前观测进行动作扩展因缺乏历史上下文利用而不足。为应对这些挑战，我们提出E-TTS，一个模块化、即插即用的具身测试时扩展框架，通过基于视觉语言验证器的历史感知迭代细化，统一了机器人操作中的推理与动作扩展。为支持推理-动作联合扩展，E-TTS以成对方式执行推理-动作联合采样与评分。为更好利用历史信息，E-TTS使用历史缓冲区存储历史数据。
tldr: 提出E-TTS框架，通过历史感知迭代细化与视觉语言验证器，统一了机器人操作中的推理与动作扩展，解决了具身任务中推理扩展机制缺失和历史信息利用不足的问题。
en_tdlr: E-TTS framework unifies reasoning and action scaling for robotic manipulation via history-aware iterative refinement with vision-language verifiers, addressing the lack of reasoning scaling mechanisms and insufficient historical context utilization in embodied tasks.
